hyperedges.summary: HL7 analyzer host query causes interface engine to fetch pending orders from LIS Core, send ORM^O01/OML^O21 in an MLLP frame, receive ACK, receive ORU^R01 result, send ACK^R01/MSA, and deliver normalized LOINC/UCUM data to LIS Core.

hyperedges.label: ASTM E1394 over RS232 result-up sequence
hyperedges.source_location: lower ASTM path (RS232, ENQ/ACK framing) sequence boundary
hyperedges.summary: ASTM analyzer initiates with ENQ, interface engine ACKs, analyzer sends H/P/O/R/C/L records, interface engine ACKs each frame using mod-256 checksum validation, analyzer ends with EOT, and interface engine delivers a normalized result to LIS Core.

nodes.label: MLLP Codec
nodes.rationale: Standalone stdlib-only codec frames and deframes SB...EB CR around verbatim HL7 payloads.

nodes.label: Incremental Self-Resynchronising MLLP Decoder
nodes.rationale: Real TCP listener needs buffered partial-frame handling that resynchronizes on corrupt streams without wedging.

nodes.label: MllpTransport
nodes.rationale: Frames on send and de-frames on receive while preserving the ADR-0004 Transport interface.

nodes.label: ACK Construction Reads Only MSH
nodes.rationale: ACK generation swaps routing and echoes control IDs without parsing result content, preserving the slice boundary.

nodes.label: Original ACK Mode
nodes.rationale: Original-mode MSA-1 uses AA, AE, or AR and is the hardware-proven mode for the v1 fleet.

nodes.label: Enhanced ACK Mode
nodes.rationale: Enhanced-mode commit ACK uses CA, CE, or CR and honors MSH-15, but remains simulator-tested only.

nodes.label: Negative ACK ERR Scope
nodes.rationale: AE/AR with populated ERR is emitted only when an MSH-parseable message is rejected by the listener.

nodes.label: Stage-1 Channel Config Instance
nodes.rationale: The MLLP port, original ACK mode, and MSH-routed identities are the Stage-1 instance of ADR-0015 channel configuration.

nodes.label: Rationale: Keep Framing and ACK Separate from ORU Parsing
nodes.rationale: The headline acceptance is ACK round-trip, while result interpretation belongs to S1.2/LIS-14.
nodes.norm_label: rationale: keep framing and ack separate from oru parsing

nodes.label: ASTM E1381 Codec and Session
nodes.rationale: Dependency-free module models ENQ/ACK/NAK/EOT session, checksum, numbered frames and termination.

nodes.label: Stop-and-Wait ACK/NAK Retransmit
nodes.rationale: Exit-gate behavior requires captured frames to ACK and corrupted frames to NAK then retransmit.

nodes.label: AstmReceiver
nodes.rationale: Receiver half ACKs ENQ, validates checksum and sequence, NAKs bad frames, and idempotently re-ACKs retransmits.

nodes.label: run_session
nodes.rationale: Deterministic in-memory full sender-to-receiver session with corruption hook, retry counts, NAKs and abort reporting.

nodes.label: AstmTransport
nodes.rationale: Frames payloads into E1381 frames and reassembles them through the existing Transport fixture contract.

nodes.label: diasys-r920-astm-result Fixture
nodes.rationale: Synthetic E1394 H-P-O-R-L record set carried over E1381 as the simulator substrate.

nodes.label: ERBA EC90 Stage-2 Vehicle Rescope
nodes.rationale: Stage-2 bench vehicle changes from DiaSys R920 to available ERBA EC90 without changing the E1381 protocol contract.

nodes.label: Rationale: Model Session, Not Just Frames
nodes.rationale: NAK plus retransmit is session behavior, so frame-only parsing cannot satisfy the exit gate.
nodes.norm_label: rationale: model session, not just frames

nodes.label: ASTM E1394 Record Parser
nodes.rationale: Dependency-free parser turns de-framed H-P-O-R-L records into a typed ASTM message tree.

nodes.label: Header-Declared ASTM Delimiters
nodes.rationale: The H record declares field, repeat, component and escape delimiters rather than inheriting HL7 MSH conventions.

nodes.label: AstmMessage Tree
nodes.rationale: Flat records imply Header, Patient, Order and Result hierarchy while retaining the ordered record list.

nodes.label: Tolerant ASTM Deviation Handling
nodes.rationale: Missing H records, orphan O/R records and unknown C/Q/M records are handled defensively instead of crashing.

nodes.label: edge-sim parse-astm CLI
nodes.rationale: CLI prints the record tree for fixture inspection.

nodes.label: Rationale: Keep E1394 Parse Separate from Normalization
nodes.rationale: LOINC/UCUM mapping is deferred to S2.4 so E1394 parsing remains a record-layer slice.
nodes.norm_label: rationale: keep e1394 parse separate from normalization

nodes.label: Tolerant HL7 v2 Parser
nodes.rationale: Dependency-free parser honors MSH delimiters and standard escapes while tolerating line endings, short segments and missing fields.

nodes.label: ORU^R01 Extraction
nodes.rationale: Walks parsed HL7 into OruReport with analyzer, patient/specimen IDs and RawObservation rows per OBX.

nodes.label: LOINC/UCUM Normalizer
nodes.rationale: Normalizer maps vendor code and unit to LOINC and UCUM while preserving raw analyzer values.

nodes.label: TerminologyMap.default
nodes.rationale: Edge analog of core vendor_code_mapping seed used when no live core DB is assumed.

nodes.label: rayto-rac050-oru-r01 Fixture
nodes.rationale: Synthetic ORU fixture with analyzer-native codes and expected normalized rows.

nodes.label: NormalizedObservation Intermediate Row
nodes.rationale: Raw code/unit sit beside normalized LOINC/UCUM and normalization status before persistence.

nodes.label: Rationale: Dependency-Free HL7 Parser
nodes.rationale: A small parser keeps the simulator portable and makes fixtures a language-neutral contract.

nodes.label: RawMessageArchive
nodes.rationale: Durable append-only store keeps raw inbound application bytes exactly as received.

nodes.label: SHA-256 Content-Addressed Store
nodes.rationale: Digest names and verifies the raw message, making archival idempotent and tamper-evident.

nodes.label: ArchiveIntegrityError
nodes.rationale: Raised when stored bytes re-hash to a different digest, surfacing corruption or tampering.

nodes.label: replay_normalized Result Digest
nodes.rationale: Canonical SHA-256 over normalized rows fingerprints the deterministic result re-derived from bytes.

nodes.label: Deterministic Round Trip
nodes.rationale: Capture, archive, reload, wire round-trip, parse and normalize in one reproducible vertical path.

nodes.label: check_against_expected
nodes.rationale: Fixture expected rows become an asserted replay checker rather than unused manifest data.

nodes.label: Rationale: Raw Wire Evidence Belongs at Edge
nodes.rationale: Core persists normalized result versions, while the edge retains the raw message evidence that produced them.
nodes.norm_label: rationale: raw wire evidence belongs at edge

nodes.label: Stage-1 First Result Milestone E2E
nodes.rationale: Automated test composes MLLP, ACK, parse, normalize and ingest DTO correspondence on EDAN H60S fixture.

nodes.label: EDAN H60S ORU Fixture
nodes.rationale: Synthetic HL7 v2.4 MLLP CBC result with EDAN-native codes and escaped units.

nodes.label: edge_sim.milestone.run_milestone
nodes.rationale: Frames inbound ORU over MLLP, acknowledges it, parses and normalizes observations, and exposes finality.

nodes.label: Ingest DTO Contract JSON Schema
nodes.rationale: Language-neutral schema pins the edge half of core ADR-0003 field-for-field correspondence.

nodes.label: Finality Safety Gate
nodes.rationale: Only OBX-11 final or updated observations flow to the ingest seam; non-final observations are held back.

nodes.label: Status Terminology Reconciliation
nodes.rationale: HL7 lifecycle finality and core normalization status coexist; finality gates flow while DTO status remains normalization state.

nodes.label: Archive Traceability for Milestone Result
nodes.rationale: Milestone result is byte-for-byte reproducible from archived raw message bytes via the same result digest.

nodes.label: Rationale: Contract Artifact Before Live Cross-Process Wire
nodes.rationale: The S1.0 substrate was undecided, so this slice pins correspondence without presupposing transport.
nodes.norm_label: rationale: contract artifact before live cross-process wire

nodes.label: Bidirectional Host Query QRD/QRF
nodes.rationale: Simulator models a QRY^R02 results query answered by ORF^R04 with returned observations.

nodes.label: QRY^R02 Query
nodes.rationale: QRD carries correlation id and subject; QRF repeats the subject as a where-filter.

nodes.label: ORF^R04 Response
nodes.rationale: Response ACKs the query, echoes the query id and serializes OBR/OBX result content.

nodes.label: Query Response Correlation
nodes.rationale: Answer is accepted only if query id, AA acknowledgement and queried subject match.

nodes.label: EDAN H60S Host Query Fixture
nodes.rationale: Synthetic QRY^R02 fixture subject SPEC-0231 is answered from the existing EDAN H60S result fixture.

nodes.label: Order Download Deferred to Stage 4
nodes.rationale: Analyzer worklist/order query direction is deferred to S4.2 even though QRD/QRF mechanics are shared.

nodes.label: Base64 OBX Re-scoped Out
nodes.rationale: Mindray labXpert Base64 payload handling is out of scope because EDAN H60S sends plaintext OBX.

nodes.label: Rationale: Simulator Substrate Before Live Bidirectional Deployment
nodes.rationale: ADR-0008 defers live bidirectional deployment post-pilot, so LIS-18 proves protocol substrate only.
nodes.norm_label: rationale: simulator substrate before live bidirectional deployment

nodes.label: Direct-Attach Edge Transport Substrate
nodes.rationale: Analyzers attach directly to analyzer-bridge native listeners; no MLLP-to-HTTP shim, serial sidecar, or standalone file poller.

nodes.label: Analyzer Bridge Native Listeners
nodes.rationale: Bridge already implements MLLP/HL7, serial, ASTM-over-TCP, file watching and outbound order/query support.

nodes.label: MessageEnvelope Convergence Seam
nodes.rationale: Every listener builds protocol, transport, sourceId, rawMessage, sourcePort and resolvedAnalyzerId into one transport-invariant seam.

nodes.label: Transport-Invariant Module Boundaries
nodes.rationale: Framer is transport-specific; parser, normalization and ingest contract are shared above MessageEnvelope.

nodes.label: Per-Analyzer Channel-Config Schema
nodes.rationale: Transport, port, ACK mode, protocol, source allow-list and mapping profile are mapped to bridge config and OpenELIS registry authority.

nodes.label: REQ-SEC-03 Two-Tier Isolation
nodes.rationale: Bridge-to-core isolation is structural; channel-to-channel isolation inside the bridge is thread-level and residual.

nodes.label: FHIR-over-HTTP Production Ingest Contract
nodes.rationale: Java bridge posts FHIR R4 transaction Bundles to OpenELIS /analyzer/fhir as the production wire.

nodes.label: NormalizedObservation Semantic Contract
nodes.rationale: Simulator DTO is the semantic invariant; FHIR Observation is the production serialization of that semantic shape.

nodes.label: OpenELIS Registry Authority
nodes.rationale: Bridge pulls analyzer registry and mapping profiles from OpenELIS and accepts sync/register updates.

nodes.label: Residual Bridge Hardening
nodes.rationale: FILE bypasses common normalizer, ASTM config key mismatch, inert filesystem DLQ, MLLP-only rate limiting and advisory allow-list are follow-ups.

nodes.label: Rationale: Reject Shim Because Native Listeners Exist
nodes.rationale: A shim would add a hop and validation object while duplicating framing and ACK surfaces already in the bridge.
nodes.norm_label: rationale: reject shim because native listeners exist

nodes.label: Multi-Context Documentation Layout
nodes.rationale: CONTEXT-MAP points to relevant CONTEXT.md files and ADRs across repo contexts.

nodes.label: Use Glossary Vocabulary
nodes.rationale: Agent output should use terms from the relevant CONTEXT.md instead of inventing synonyms.

nodes.label: ADR Conflict Flagging
nodes.rationale: Contradictions with existing ADRs must be surfaced explicitly.

nodes.label: Plane.so Issue Tracker
nodes.rationale: Plane is the issue and PRD system; agents should not use GitHub issues as the front door.

nodes.label: Bundled Plane CLI
nodes.rationale: Plane CLI is invoked by absolute path and requires PLANE_API_KEY and PLANE_WORKSPACE.

nodes.label: scripts/slice.py Front Door
nodes.rationale: slice.py performs filtered next/status/claim/heartbeat/release operations subprocess-side to avoid raw backlog dumps.

nodes.label: Plane Issue Body as Description
nodes.rationale: scripts/plane_issue.py renders markdown into the work-item description; comments are reserved for progress logs.

nodes.label: Triage State Transition
nodes.rationale: Triage roles are realized as Plane workflow state updates, not labels.

nodes.label: Dedicated Slice Worktree
nodes.rationale: Each Plane slice uses ../lis-control-<key> on branch <key>-<slug>; primary checkout stays on its branch.

nodes.label: Plane Claim Ledger
nodes.rationale: slice.py claim creates assignment plus TTL claim comments for cooperative locking.

nodes.label: Slice Loop Iteration
nodes.rationale: Select, set up, sync and claim, work one increment, integrate, log and finish or loop.

nodes.label: Submodule Two-Level PR
nodes.rationale: Submodule changes land in component repo first, then the umbrella bumps the submodule pin.

nodes.label: Slice Branch to PR to main
nodes.rationale: Every slice reaches main through a PR; direct commits or pushes to main are forbidden.
nodes.norm_label: slice branch to pr to main

nodes.label: Triage Roles as Plane Workflow States
nodes.rationale: needs-triage, needs-info, ready-for-agent, ready-for-human and wontfix map to Plane states.
nodes.norm_label: triage roles as plane workflow states

nodes.label: LIS-10 Compliance Scaffold
nodes.rationale: Stage-0 paperwork-and-safety foundation for privacy, validation, threat and traceability artifacts.

nodes.label: Validation Master Plan Outline
nodes.rationale: Plan for proving the system works correctly via ISO 15189 IQ/OQ/PQ and staged verification.

nodes.label: NPC Checklist
nodes.rationale: Practical registration and Data Privacy Act compliance checklist.

nodes.label: STRIDE Threat Model
nodes.rationale: Threat analysis maps defenses to requirement IDs and test levels.

nodes.label: Seeded Traceability Matrix
nodes.rationale: Requirement-to-test-to-evidence master table and authoritative REQ-* registry.

nodes.label: Decisions Register
nodes.rationale: Deduplicated human-in-the-loop decisions, options, owners and blockers.

nodes.label: Human Decision Gate
nodes.rationale: LIS-10 remains ready-for-human because ownership, DPO, signatures, counsel and filings are not agent-producible.

nodes.label: Rationale: Validate Deltas on a Known Base
nodes.rationale: The scaffold lets later stages prove small controlled changes instead of an unauditable go-live leap.
nodes.norm_label: rationale: validate deltas on a known base

nodes.label: MPL-2.0 File-Level Copyleft
nodes.rationale: MPL obligations attach to individual MPL-covered files rather than the whole repository.

nodes.label: MPL Distribution Trigger
nodes.rationale: Private repository use triggers nothing; distribution to customer sites requires source availability for MPL-covered files.

nodes.label: OpenELIS Core MPL Component
nodes.rationale: OpenELIS-Global-2 fork is in repo and pinned, carrying MPL-2.0 obligations.

nodes.label: openelis-analyzer-bridge MPL Component
nodes.rationale: Analyzer bridge is in use under MPL-2.0 with modified warranty clauses and tracked as upstream code.

nodes.label: Compliance Scaffold Index
nodes.rationale: README maps the compliance folder deliverables, status and provenance.

nodes.label: Traceability Matrix as Spine
nodes.rationale: The matrix owns canonical REQ-* IDs referenced by the VMP, NPC checklist and threat model.

nodes.label: Leadership Decision Gate Cleared
nodes.rationale: ADR-0007 and ADR-0008 resolved regulatory ownership, DPO, signatories, interface engine, stack, fleet and license decisions.

nodes.label: Third-Party Reuse Hold
nodes.rationale: Components blocked from direct reuse until license or compliance issues are cleared.

nodes.label: HOLD-001 Cleared
nodes.rationale: Direct reuse of openelis-analyzer-bridge is permitted under modified MPL-2.0.

nodes.label: Analyzer Bridge as Upstream Code
nodes.rationale: Private mirror is byte-identical to DIGI-UW upstream and not LabSolution IP.

nodes.label: Modified Warranty NOASSERTION
nodes.rationale: GitHub reports NOASSERTION because healthcare disclaimers modify warranty sections, not the use grant.

nodes.label: HITL Decisions Register
nodes.rationale: Consolidates deferred human decisions surfaced across VMP, NPC checklist, threat model and matrix.

nodes.label: Leadership Decisions Resolved
nodes.rationale: DEC-01 through DEC-08 leadership gates are resolved by ADR-0006, ADR-0007 and ADR-0008.

nodes.label: Genuinely Open Human Items
nodes.rationale: Remaining needs include counsel confirmations, clause currency, named-person paperwork, filings and signatures.

nodes.label: SD-0 Build-Now Not Pilot-Gating
nodes.rationale: ASTM/serial stack can be built now while ERBA/ASTM remains post-pilot for go-live.

nodes.label: DEC-09 Key Management Root of Trust
nodes.rationale: TLS private keys, at-rest encryption keys and service credentials need custody and rotation decisions.
nodes.norm_label: dec-09 key management root of trust

nodes.label: DEC-17 Vendor PHI Boundary
nodes.rationale: Determines whether vendor middleware or support paths transit PHI and require DPA/DSA flow-down.

nodes.label: M3 Gate: PIP with Physical Custody
nodes.rationale: LabSolution becomes a PIP with physical custody when it aggregates PHI on its own central node.
nodes.norm_label: m3 gate: pip with physical custody

nodes.label: M3 Aggregation DPS Registration
nodes.rationale: LabSolution must register its own aggregation data processing system with NPC before the M3 spoke.

nodes.label: Head PIC-to-PIP DPA
nodes.rationale: Lab-to-LabSolution Rule X DPA governs aggregation processing for M3.

nodes.label: M3 Breach Apparatus
nodes.rationale: LabSolution must stand up incident policy, DBRT, incident documentation and annual reporting for the central node.

nodes.label: M3 Datacenter Physical, BCP and Key Custody Controls
nodes.rationale: Central node requires physical security, in-PH backup/DR, central key custody and encryption/TLS controls.
nodes.norm_label: m3 datacenter physical, bcp and key custody controls

nodes.label: M3 Threat Model and Revalidation Delta
nodes.rationale: M3 adds sync and central-node threat surfaces and is validated as a change-control delta on M1.
nodes.norm_label: m3 threat model and revalidation delta

nodes.label: M1 Zero-PHI-Access Premise
nodes.rationale: The M1 neither-PIC-nor-PIP position holds only if LabSolution never accesses, stores or receives live PHI.

nodes.label: NPC Registration Privacy Checklist
nodes.rationale: Data-privacy spine for RA 10173 compliance and NPC registration before go-live.

nodes.label: Sensitive Personal Information
nodes.rationale: Clinical LIS stores patient identifiers and diagnostic results, which are sensitive personal information under RA 10173.

nodes.label: NPCRS Registration
nodes.rationale: NPC Circular 2022-04 governs online registration through NPCRS; Circular 17-01 is obsolete.

nodes.label: DPO Governance
nodes.rationale: DPO appointment, mandate, independence, reporting and privacy accountability structure are required.

nodes.label: RoPA and PIA
nodes.rationale: Processing activities and privacy impacts must be documented over patient/order/specimen/result/audit flows.

nodes.label: Lawful Basis and Data-Subject Rights
nodes.rationale: Sensitive PHI processing basis, privacy notice, DSR mechanisms, minors/incapacity and erasure-retention reconciliation need policy and counsel.

nodes.label: Breach Management
nodes.rationale: Breach procedure, notifiability criteria, detection, register, DBRT and tabletop exercise support REQ-PRIV-02.

nodes.label: Data Processing Agreements
nodes.rationale: PHI-touching parties require DPAs or DSAs depending on PIC/PIP and data-sharing roles.

nodes.label: Retention and Secure Disposal
nodes.rationale: Retention schedule and disposal must reconcile ISO 15189/RA 4688 records with RA 10173 erasure rights.

nodes.label: Currency Reconfirmation
nodes.rationale: NPC process, breach window, DSR SLA, DOH AO and ISO edition must be checked at filing or go-live.

hyperedges.label: Instrument ingestion patterns
hyperedges.source_location: four analyzer/middleware boxes feed the interface engine

hyperedges.label: HL7 v2 MLLP/TCP order-down and result-up sequence
hyperedges.source_location: upper HL7 v2 path (MLLP / TCP) sequence boundary
hyperedges.summary: HL7 analyzer host query causes interface engine to fetch pending orders from LIS Core, send ORM^O01/OML^O21 in an MLLP frame, receive ACK, receive ORU^R01 result, send ACK^R01/MSA, and deliver normalized LOINC/UCUM data to LIS Core.

hyperedges.label: ASTM E1394 over RS232 result-up sequence
hyperedges.source_location: lower ASTM path (RS232, ENQ/ACK framing) sequence boundary
hyperedges.summary: ASTM analyzer initiates with ENQ, interface engine ACKs, analyzer sends H/P/O/R/C/L records, interface engine ACKs each frame using mod-256 checksum validation, analyzer ends with EOT, and interface engine delivers a normalized result to LIS Core.

hyperedges.label: Protocol evidence and implementation rationale
hyperedges.source_location: subtitle, ASTM build note, and bottom evidence blocks
hyperedges.summary: The diagram states tolerant HL7 parsing with raw capture for replay, ASTM E1381/E1394 implementation constraints, and example HL7 and ASTM frames including EDAN H60S, ERBA EC90, WBC/HGB observations, and an ASTM sodium R record.
